name: Tussenjaartje want t is nodig
summary: Veel gezien, veel geskied en weer een tijdje thuis gewoond

all_steps.name: Antwerp & Delft
all_steps.description: Zomertje afstuderen (1), wegverhuizen *sniksnik*(2), teveel mealpreppen voor festivals en concertjes doen(3-7), CERN bezoeken en doen alsof we er werken(8-10), op en neer naar de olympische spelen(11-13), KAPOT VEEL echt werken deze keer(14), weekendjes in nederland voor allerlei domme dingen waarbij je je pijn kan doen(15-17), mijn rijbewijs eindelijk halen en we zijn klaar om aan het jaar te beginnen. Natuurlijk ben ik pas vanavond begonnen met inpakken... (18) dat was even stressen maar ik ben wel enthousiast!!

all_steps.name: Hong kong - Kowloon
all_steps.description:
Na 14 uur vliegen komen papa en ik moe maar blij (ik allessinds) aan op het vliegveld, en op de tocht naar het hotel beginnen we al eigenaardige dingen tegen te komen. Van kindjes op rijdende valiezen tot bierreclames op wolkenkrabbers (2), mensen die schaamteloos met vol geluid aan doomscrollen op de metro en duizenden voetmassage-tenten in elke straat.

Vandaag hebben we in de wijk bij central een skateparkje gevonden (ja ik heb mijn bord mee en nee dat is niet dom). Na een zomer amper skaten kan ik wel echt niks meer :( (3).  Daarna zijn we naar de beurs gegaan om ons standje van de week voor te bereiden. 'S avonds ben ik met collegas van mijn pa een vlooienmarkt (4-6) vol fake chinese dingen doorgewandeld en we hebben het goed uitgehangen: karaoke gezongen, zelf de verkoper uitgehangen en een valies ingekropen (lifehack: doe alsof je bij een circus werkt en je moet checken of dat lukt voor een act, dan laten ze het toe)

all_steps.name: Hong Kong - Asia world expo
all_steps.description:
After a few days full time at the gem fair at AsiaWorld-expo (1) I understand a bit more about the gemstone business. I try to help out here and there in our booth but I am mostly observing. 

Everyone has their own specialization, one business even focuses on overpricing junk haha ​​(2), and many Indians are greenwashing by namedropping SDG's. Furthermore everyone keeps saying that I should DEFINITELY not join the family business but proceeds by offering me an internship 30 seconds later. Of course I keep blabbing without a filter, but people find it funny (especially the Mexicans today).

In the evening I also saw some of Hong Kong by walking around and going out for dinner in nice Asian restaurants 😋. The weather is hot and humid, it even started raining like crazy tonight, but Hong Kong has creative solutions! (3)

all_steps.name: Hong Kong - Soho
all_steps.description:
First week is over already! 

The gem fair on Wednesday and Thursday was not busy, which gave me time to explore the city some more beforehand. I went to the old prison and some nice neighbourhoods on hongkong island(1). At night the city really lights up (and taxi drivers play on their phones while driving apparently) (2).
 Friday was a busy day packing everything up at the fair, it was EXTRA tiring because I had just gotten sick (classic story of the brain's confusion caused by AIRCOS). We treated ourselves (or well, dad treated me) with a delicious meal in a Japanese restaurant afterwards. :) Saturday I stayed in bed until late to recover, and we went to the other jewellery fair after to say hello to Jacques and his collection of interestingly named antique artifacts (3).

Luckily feeling better already today! Nevertheless, today will be remembered as Screw Up Sunday: We wanted to go for the dragon's back hike (bag with raingear, drinks,... prepared) butttt didn't time it well with the TOEFL exam I had later today (nailed that btw). So we went back to drop our stuff at the hotel and went to the art museum, which kind of blew (except for the collection of tobacco snuffboxes). Then we wanted to go play boardgames (the only time I didn't bring my cards) and ended up in some sketchy appartement. So we started walking to a coffeebar and it started raining, very hard... (4) Later we skipped on dinner together since dad was gonna be late for his flight otherwise. I ate dinner with Cynthia later, and all in all it was still a nice day!

all_steps.name: Guangzhou
all_steps.description:
Daytrip to mainland China! 

I decided to go visit Guangzhou, the third largest city of China (18M people & 60M+ in the area!). Took a high speed bullet train (350km/h :0) and got there within the hour (17).
I also brought my camera today and started shooting pictures for the first time in years! (1-13) very happy with the results.

First thing I noticed upon arrival is the abundance of cameras everywhere pfff, and literally no one speaks English. But other than that I was pleasantly surprised: the city is super green, there is a stream that flows through the center + it didn't feel that busy. It is actually a nice city, except for the commies keeping tabs on ya offcourse. 

I started by taking the metro to Sun Yat Sen's memorial hall & learned a brainwashed version of the Chinese revolution. Then walked up the mountain in Yuexiu park before taking a taxi to the Liwan lake. Super local there, saw some old guys play cards and I ended up hanging out with a couple of even older guitar players (12). 
After that I got semi-lost in a super interesting residential area before continuing to the more commercial, touristy neighbourhoud. I noticed there were more flags/nationalist symbols. Grabbed a coffee and the barista (finally someone who speaks english) recommended an ultra local place to eat dinner (15&16). Went there, met some Turkish Chinese guys on the way, played fifa with them for a bit and was almost late for the train back! My tardiness did lead to meeting a South African who was just as late while running to the station, so all good :)

Daytrip to Macau today.

It's this weird administrative region of China (like HK but then 100%). It was a Portuguese colony for about 400 years, which has left a lot of Western influences (cobblestone streets for example(2&6)). I noticed more people speak English, portuguese must be an official second language because all the signposts are bilingual and architecture is Southern European fused with asian elements.
Also, in the last 30 years it has become Chinese Las Vegas for some reason. So it's a weird mixture of different vibes, which is cool for a bit but not amazing or anything.

To summarize: it's a couple of churches & old ruins with one million Chinese tourists in the old centre (1-3) next to ultra extravagant casinos and hotels  (10) contrasted with trashy apartment buildings that are falling apart (9). So my impression was meh, would not recommend.

all_steps.name: Hong Kong - Islands district
all_steps.description:
Last day in Hongkong, planned on FINALLY doing the dragon back hike and also Lantau island. Busy day so I woke up very early, but... it was raining all morning.

Still managed to go to Lantau island when it cleared up later in the day. I did the classics like the ngong ping 360 cable car (2), the big Buddha (1) and the Po Lin monastery first (3-5). Then I left the center and started randomly wandering around, hoping to encounter a village or something more off the beaten track. I followed the road downhill, until I noticed a small trail off the side. I looked it up and there were hiking trails in the area!!! (6-11) So no dragon back, but I ended up doing a nice hike up the mountain through some dense vegetation. And I had the whole trail to myself because Lantau is not your go to hike area. It was awesome :)

Back in Hong Kong I picked up my stuff at the hotel and had the first me moment of the trip: gave my wallet at the reception to check something, and left it there while going up to my room oops. Two minutes later get a call on the room phone, so I immediately go pick it up. And then the most stuck up regulation thing ever happened: same guy who took my wallet and had called me asked for my passport to "check if it was my wallet" before giving it back haha.

Then went out for dinner with Cynthia again (thai this time) and left for the airport very early. So early that I killed time with a little skatesesh (yeah security didn't mind 😎), ended it with the best backside flip I have ever landed. Later on security was annoying after all and I had to check in my skateboard, but no worries packed it up and we're off!
Next up: South Korea.

*update* 15 minutes later and the smooth sailing already came to a halt: my pants ripped. Extra ventilation I guess... (12)

all_steps.name: Seoul - Gagnam & Hongdae
all_steps.description:
Arrived in Seoul city around 10 A.M, dropped off my stuff and immediately joined Sjors in the student/party neighbourhood (2). Ate some Korean food (weird cold noodle soup, not a fan) and got a little taste of the city there too. There are cool bars and shops everywhere and EVERYONE is dressed well wtf. We thrifted a bit and I noticed how much Korean society relies on trust: the metro doesn't have gates at the check in, there are water fountains & clean toilets for free everywhere, unmanned shops, etc. No way you could do that in Europe...

We joined the rest of the group at a huge foodmarket afterwards and got semi harassed by "documentary" filmers(4), before splitting up so I could go check in. Seoul is HUGE: it takes about an hour to get from Gagnam (where I'm sleeping) to neighbourhoods in the center, and that is considered close.

Anyways, chilled a bit (super duper tired after the early morning flight) and joined Sjors later near his house for some drinks. We ate pizza and barhopped in Haebangchon, which is a very fun neighbourheid on top of the mountain. Found a Korean Duvel there! (8) And tasted a couple of new beers: Cass is definitely the best local one. Ow, also discovered milkis today: it's a weirdly tasty blend of soda and milk (9).

all_steps.name: Seoul - Gyeongbokgung palace
all_steps.description:
I caught up on some sleep, called char (finally) and ate breakfast in Gagnam alone. Then I got confused in the subway and that's why the day started very late. 

We took the afternoon, a.k.a my start of the day, to visit the Gyeongbokgung palace (2), while wearing the traditional Hanbok (1&2) of course (thus getting free entry haha). There were a couple of fighter jets pelotons on a drill passing above us while we were there, which was really sick (3).

Then Sjors and I went to the Ikseon dong old Hanok village, which is this cute neighbourhoud of traditional Korean houses with loads of places to eat etc. 

Ended the day partying near Hongdae with Sjors &co and Alex (the American from the hostel). I ended up going to bed around 6:30, so it was a pretty fun night :)

all_steps.name: Seoul - Hanok village & SOHO
all_steps.description:
We started the day late again, this time with a valid reason though. Alix (who we met going out yesterday), Sjors and I met at a flee market swarming with elderly people (3-6). Grabbed breakfast from a super local sandwhich/dumpling place and ate near the river (7-10).

We split our ways after that, and Sjors and I went to the new uniqlo shop and the DDS design museum (very cool Zara Hadid building). 

Went to a Korean BBQ place that looked cool the other day in the Hanok village and went for drinks again after in a cool place that brew their own beers.

all_steps.name: Seoul - Namsan tower & museums
all_steps.description:
Last day in Seoul so I woke up at 8! 
Started the day with a hike to namsan tower to enjoy the view (2-5). Had bagels for brunch at a place Sjors knew and went thrifting around that area too.

Around midday we went to the Korean War memorial & museum, which was awesome, and then to the National museum, where the expo was not that nice but the architecture was(1,6-8).

Spent the end of the afternoon at Yeouido park on the other side of the river with the intention of watching the sunset. Many locals had the same idea, but the weather didn't allow it. Went back to Haebangchon for pizza and drinks, and met up with Alix again there too. It's definitely our favourite neighbourhood hihi.

After a crazy, busy and satisfying last day I packed my bags and left for the airport at 3 A.M.

DAY 1:
Had a little trouble at the gate (AT 5 A.M pff) because my bags were too heavy and I preferred not checking anything in, so I was the last one entering the plane oops. And in the hassle I forgot my charger, powerbank and earbuds at the airport >:(.
I did meet a friendly computer scientist from Germany in the process, who I shared a cab to Hanoi with. I booked a hotel for the first night, and met some Belgians there instantly. Spent the day walking around with Tim in the old quarter & presidential district, passed by the Ngoc Son buddhist temple on the lake & the skateshop to ask for the best spots. Just went out to eat with Tim and then I chilled at the hotel because I was VERY tired from the overnight flight. Called Jo, mom & Wannes and fixed some uni stuff.

DAY 2:
Lazy day today, checked out late. I walked around a bit and got used to the crazy traffic. It's true what they say about Vietnam: the only traffic rule is "try not to die" (5). So you just gotta keep walking and let traffic flow around you. I managed pretty quickly and even skated through it, feeling like Jaden Smith when he arrives in China in Karate Kid haha.
After checking in to the Central Backpackers hostel today I chilled a bit and worked again, before going out for dinner with random italians/german/spanish I met. Then went to Happy hour, you get free beer at the hostel for an hour, drinking on the rooftop. I met two groups of israelis (they're everywhere wtf) and one them was going to Chabad for New Years too!

DAY 3:
Compensating for the recent laziness today! Woke up early and skated all over the city: Bach ma buddhist temple & the rest of the Old Quarter first, ate local again (starting to really like thos pho en bún soups(6)), visited Hoa Lo prison relic (French colonial), accidentally passed by Chùa Quán Sứ buddhist temple, went to the confucionist Temple of literature (which was the first national school in 1076 and a milestone for education during the history of vietnam) and then I wanted to go to the Vietnam military history museum. But apparently that's right by lenin plaza, the most famous skate spot... So I ended up skating with a local crew (8) for two hours.
Then it was time for Rosh Hashana dinner! (9) The food wasn't great but the vibe was fun, and we went out with a group after. That's where I met Mikey and Ron, who I decided to continue traveling with at 2 A.M haha. Now the question is: Where do we go from here?

Took the minibus to Ninh Binh at 11:30 with mikey and ron, ate soup while driving (0/10 would not recommend). Had a circular discussion about what hostel we should book. Ended up booking a 6 dollar private room in a BEAUTIFUL homestay (3).

Just like all over the country, there are cute little altars everywhere (2): with offers for both fun buddha (alcohol) and strict buddha (food).

We chilled at the pool for the rest of the day,  reading and falling asleep for 3 hours. Then, in the early evening, we decided to rent a bike and go to Thai Vy temple to make something of our day. We ate with Laura and partied in Tam Coc after (on the karaoke bus of course).

Officially drank the cheapest beer in the world: 7000vnd for a 0.5L bottle, which is about 0.26 euros! (4)

all_steps.name: Tam Coc - Hoa Lu Ancient Capital
all_steps.display_name: Tam Coc - Hoa Lu Ancient Capital
all_steps.description:
DAY 2:
We split up because Ron is scared of motorbikes or smt, rented motorbikes and explored the environment after a lazy morning.

We went to Hoa Lu ancient capital & a village nearby, met Gyat: a kid who wanted to practice his english with us and dragged us on a crazy offroad hike. 

Ended the day with a dinner with big group (partly from the hostel partly from yesterday's party), and we decided to extend our stay for a day because it's so amazing here.

all_steps.name: Tam Coc - Mua Cave hike
all_steps.description:
DAY 3:
Bike tour, mua cave & viewpoint, buffalo cave & locals thinking I am Harry Potter, Den voi temple, bird valley boat tour but oops we're too late so we did a cave instead. Dinner and party in Tam Cóc again.

all_steps.name: Tam coc - Thrang Anh boat tour
all_steps.display_name: Tam coc - Thrang Anh boat tour
all_steps.description:
It's the fourth and last day in Tam Cóc, Mikey and Ron left for Hanoi around 12:30 so it was time to split up :(( 

I just relaxed all morning by the pool, chilling with the new people in the hostel. Unfortunately no one is going my way so no travel buddies for the moment.  

In the afternoon I rented a bike again and went for the Trangh Anh boat tour (finally lol) with the australian girls from yesterday. (1-4)

Rushed back to have time for a shower and dinner before getting on the bus to Ha Giang at 7 p.m. It's a sleeper bus, so I'll have a comfortable ride. (5)

all_steps.name: Ha giang loop
all_steps.description:
Finished the Ha Giang loop! A 370 km biketour in 4 days, amazing but exhausting.
In hindsight, doing it with an easyrider was definitely a good choice. There were unpaved parts of the road, crazy turns and debris from the landslides everywhere!

Day 1:
we left around 9 (I had only slept 5 hours because my bus arrived at 4) and the vibes were great. I did lose my shoe in the bus so I had to do the tour in sandals. The group is fun (1): especially Jack and Charlie. Two aussie brothers who swear with stupid words like dingus and nuffy, and start drinking at 11 A.M. The easyriders are hilarious too, especially Kai, Binh and Cheu. They keep giving you lollypops and slapping me on the ass. Cheu (the người chuyển giớ guy) even tried to hook me up with a Vietnamese girl hahaha. Got stung by a bee and cut my foot with the vietnamese batminton game thingy, so losing my shoe has had its repercussions. All good tho! Ended the night early after a dip in the pool and learning vietnamese pool.

DAY 2:
Today the surroundings got more beautiful by the second. We arrived at the highest point of the region and climbed a little path to a protruding rock, very instagramable spot, the photographer wasn't very happy when I peed off of it haha. A girl in our group fell of the bike, which was kind of scary but she's totally fine. Later her bike got a flat tire too so she was pretty unlucky. But again: all good! Did karaoke at night, we get free happy water and the drivers try to get us drunk constantly. 

DAY 3: 
This was the best day, we went through the smallest villages and stopped at a spot to go on a bamboo boat ride (2). The boat dropped us off at a cave where me and charlie found a cliffjumping spot. Sketchy way up, but nice jump down and I genuinely believe we were the first humans to ever set foot up there. I backflipped off the ±6m jump, felt sick. Afterwards I convinced my driver to let me drive for a bit. Then I got super drunk tonight oops. Naturally we did karaoke again, in Vietnam there's no party without it. I tried the Vietnamese Tobacco bong and tried to learn chinese chess. And the French girls from my table at Rosh Hasjana are here too!

DAY 4:
Hard morning haha, rice wine headaches are no myth. The first stop today was at a waterfall swimming spot, it's a classic stop so it was very crowded but still nice. There was also cliffjumping, but it wasn't as cool as the other day. At a coffee pitstop I saw the Israelis from my table at the Rosh Hasjana dinner, what are the odds haha. We had our last family dinner for lunch (3) (been eating the same meal twice a day here, it is tasty though). We arrived back at hostel around 16:30. I picked up my lost shoe!! and we went for pizza afterwards.

 I just left for an 8h sleeperbus to Sapa (15), it's the same bus as the Aussies so no goodbye yet :)

all_steps.name: Sapa - rice field trekking
all_steps.description:
Arrived at Pang's house around 2 A.M in a village outside of Sapa. It's a super hard bed but I slept very well and had CRAZY dreams.  

I am staying with Pang and her family for two nights. They are part of the Hmong ethnic group and she's explained a lot about her background. I met three French girls who are staying here too.

Pang is pregnant, so her aunt aya guided our trek today. We walked about 13km through the bamboo forest (4&5), rice terraces (1) and had lunch in a village nearby. The destruction from the taiphoon a month ago is still very visible (3): the main road is still blocked, and landslides obliterated houses on the way. Aya told us how she's lost her mom and 3 other relatives in the landslides. People here are very resilient though, we saw them building new houses and Aya was even cracking jokes about her mom (we started naming bamboo trees after ourselves, she pointed to an old near dead one and said "that one's my mom"). There are loads of dogs here (6) and we picked up a friend along the way.

I chilled in the Hammock (with my new dogfriend) and played with the kids after the walk leading me to meet Ben, a new guy who is staying here. Then it was time for dinner, the food was really good and we talked some more with Pang. She is super funny too, using French words she picked up from tourists like "flemme" and "leche-cul". She invited us to a festival/death ritual thing her dad is organizing in the village tomorrow.

all_steps.name: Sapa- Xa Muong Hoa town feast
all_steps.display_name: Sapa- Xa Muong Hoa town feast
all_steps.description:
The festival from Pang's family was a crazy experience: it's a sort of ritual 6 years after someone's death, they wear traditional clothes (Pang gave me some that she had made) and kill a buffalo in honour of the deceased. During all of this everyone gets wasted on rice wine playing a drinking game (at 10:30 A.M) and sjamaans do ritual dances. Then we eat the buffalo for lunch, which felt kind of weird (although it was tasty). 

It was a super authentic experience, a few others had brought their guests to the party but there were barely any tourists. We did meet Eva though and when we couldn't handle any more alcohol, we all left for a little walk guided by Julia and another local kid. We met Lala and played pool on the way, swam in the waterfall and then headed back to Pang's. After our last dinner together, Pang gave us goodbye bracelets and Ben and I left for a hostel in Sapa. Big day tomorrow, after a lot of doubts we're headed for Fansipan mountain...

all_steps.name: Sapa - Fansipan mountain
all_steps.description:
Conquered mount Fansipan, the roof of Indochina, with our private mountain goat Bình!! 

3143m high, 22.4km walk, 1900m elevation gain, 6 hours up & 4 hours down.

We woke up at 6:10, took a taxi to the ranger station and started the tram ton trail pass around 7:30. We got lucky with the weather in the morning, but it shifts very quickly which made parts of the trail foggy and rainy. We had lunch at the 2800m basecamp and arrived at the top around 13:30, which was unfortunately covered in fog. There's this temple complex at the top, and right when you think you've arrived there's a freaking annoying 600 step staircase leading up to the top. 

Exhausted but satisfied!

I took a shower, went out for (a very rushed) dinner with Ben and left for Catba at 21:30.

all_steps.name: Cat Ba island
all_steps.description:
DAY 1:
I slept like a rose through nearly the whole trip,  waking up realizing the bus was on a boat. It's a super sociable hostel, so I quickly meet people to have breakfast with. On the way I see freaking Anna again, and I would proceed to see three more people from earlier in the trip. Everyone does the same stuff haha. Chilled and went to the beach, called Wannes (2) and worked for uni applications the rest of the day, surprised at the fact that I am still able to walk after yesterday.

DAY 2:
Arthur and Romeo (3) from the room are super chill, and we decide to do a boat trip (4) together the next day. It felt unnecessarily fancy being on a cruise like that, but it was really cool. We went biking in the national park, kayaking and swam to an island with monkeys. At night we went out in Oasis and met a lot of other French people (they are everywhere on this island), including Julia and Coline who are also pretty cool. We went for a walk on the pier after and Mona fell in this huge hole (5), which was scary at first but she was fine so it was hilarious. Arriving back around 5 A.M at the hostel Arthur broke the bathroom window (6), but he got away with it haha. 

DAY 3
We started the day late after yesterday night, and Arthur, Romeo and I picked up a German girl to join our motorbike tour (7). First we went for breakfast at a place where they were going to kill a chicken for us. Then we started driving around, passing by a deserted beach that got ruined by the typhoon, a cave that used to be a field hospital during the Vietnam war and ultimately deciding to stay for one more night. We met up with the French girls at the beach and started drinking early prior to a crazy night out, again. Might have eaten dog from the dancing Banh Mi stands but we're not sure.

DAY 4:
Last day so we're just chilling and waiting for the bus. It's raining anyways and we are hungover, so we just went out for brunch with the gang, I skated a bit and said goodbye before realizing I accidentally booked my bus for next week. Oops. Well, I guess I am staying one more night. Had a nice dinner with the girls and two replacement French dudes who were super chill before going to sleep a bit earlier tonight.

DAY 5 (oops):
Early wake up today, saying goodbye again and working a bit before leaving for Hanoi at 12.

I haven't updated for a while, probably because the last couple of days were not very interesting.

After arriving in Hanoi, I chilled at the hostel a bit and skated around the city to get stuff done. Fixed my camera (jeej I can make pictures again, and even fixed my second lens), did laundry, exchanged money, etc. 
Then I met Romeo and Arthur for dinner in a fancy indian restaurant and we went out afterwards. I really like Hanoi by day, but I am not into the local party scene. There's a weird vibe & a lot of motorbikes harass you into buying stuff.

The next day I was off to Da Nang, I took a shamefully short flight and a well deserved streak of bad weather followed the next couple of days. The hostel in Da Nang wasn't located very well, and the city feels like an American beach city with it's big roads and skyscrapers.  Even though the people were fun (and "clubbing" was hilarious) I decided to rebook and spend my last couple of days in Hoi An. This ended up being a good choice!

I stayed in Hoi An for 2 nights, and despite the rain I had a really good time (the pictures are meh though). The city is filled with tailors and jewellers, loads of markets and the lanterns everywhere are beautiful at night.  On the first day I met some Belgians and did a cooking class with them. I managed to do some work after, took a nap and chilled at their hostel in the evening. I saw Mona from Cat Ba and the Israelis from Rosh Hashana again!

The next day I slept in, met my roomies (2 germans and an italian) and spent the whole day walking around the city with them.

After an uneventful (but relaxing) couple of days and an eventful taxi ride to the airport (the driver was in party mode) I am off for Cambodia! 
Of course there is not one cloud in sight in Hoi An today...

all_steps.name: Siem reap - birthday wakesesh
all_steps.description:
The hostel I am staying at in Siem Reap has (multiple) nice pools, so I relaxed all afternoon reading at the pool. I met a fun group of people to go out for dinner with and we went dancing after, where some israelis joined our group. Apparently I brought the rain with me from Hoi An, oops... It was pooring harder than I have ever seen.

The day after I woke up late, and decide to spend my birthday at the wakepark with part of yesterday's group. We booked a two hour session, and met the only professional wakeboarder of Cambodja. Bunleap is completely self taught and helped me out a lot when I started trying a backflip. I was grinding the trick for hours and got hyped up by a random guy on the sidelines. Turns out he was the boss of the park, and when my time was up he said I could continue for free! 

Unfortunately I didn't land it, and I was starting to get tired which made it a bit dangerous. My friends all left but I decided to take a long break and try again later. At that point there was no one in the park anymore, Bunleap was done riding, solely focused on coaching me haha. And it worked!! I fricking landed a backflip twice in a row. I stayed for drinks with the staff, they got me a surprise cake and the boss offered me a free birthday session for tomorrow (since you're not supposed to pay on your birthday and I did). I got a lift back to the hostel from the barman and went to sleep. Again, exhausted but satisfied :)

The next day I woke up insanely sore and my hand was blistered from holding the handle haha. I pushed myself so hard and waked for like 5/6 hour straight but it was worth it!!

all_steps.name: Siem reap - Angkor Temples
all_steps.description:
As if I was not going to be tired enough from wakeboarding yesterday, I only slept about 3 hours tonight. I woke up at 4 for the Angkor temple sunrise tour. We visited Angkor Wat, the jungle temple, the temple run one and a couple of others with a nice guide called Chong. I learned a lot with the "Chong family" today and arrived back at the hostel around 14h for a well deserved nap. I decided not to go wakeboarding again because muscles I didn't even know existed hurt all day. Chilled for the rest of the day, and met Ashley who might do her diving license on Phuket too next week!

The next day Jasmine, Nativ, Ashley and I rented  motorcycles and explored the temple complex some more (at a normal hour this time). There was a firebreathing show and party at the wakepark, but Ashley and I ended up getting a bit lost on the way back and exploring instead. We tried to sneak into a university lecture and saw some demon cows outside of the city. When we got back to the hostel, we randomly crossed a party tuktuk that turned out to be the wakepark's. After bringing back the bike, I joined the tuktuk and pubcrawled around town.

all_steps.name: Banteay Srei - failed trip to the north?
all_steps.display_name: Banteay Srei - failed trip to the north?
all_steps.description:
After an ultimate day of nothingness chilling at the hostel yesterday, Nativ and I left for the North with the intention of discovering untouched nature and jungle. Little did we know, there is zero infrastructure for tourists outside of the city. 

We stayed in a homestay with bungalows about two hours away from Siem Reap, the 'reception' didn't speak a word of english and there was only one place to eat about 15 minutes away. It was raining all day and there was a blackout half the time so we didn't do much, but it was relaxing. 

The next morning we woke up around 6 A.M to take a sketchy tuktuk to a nearby hike through the jungle with ancient carvings on a riverbed (Kbal Spean). The scenery was nice and it was a cool walk, so all in all we did get what we wanted from the North. Around 10 A.M our friend Kim picked us up with his taxi and we were off to Siem Reap again.

all_steps.name: Siem reap - kampong phluk floating village
all_steps.display_name: Siem reap - kampong phluk floating village
all_steps.description:
I took a nap after arriving back at the hostel and booked the afternoon floating village tour on Tonle Sap lake. Since the Khmer Rouge rule, closed off communities of fishermen have been living on the lake permanently (1.5M people! Almost 10% of the population).  Since the lake size varies from 2500km² to 15000km² throughout the dry/rain season, part of the village is flooded for half the year. We went to the village market on land and then on a boat tour to visit the fishermen. Arriving at the center of the lake I jumped in the water and watched the sunset :) 

Afterwards I went out with Nativ for our last dinner and tomorrow I am off to Thailand.

Beach sunsets, amazing sea life and prostitutes. Phuket has it all...

I was on the first ever direct flight from Siem Reap to Phuket so there was a welcome committee and we got goodie bags. Nevertheless, I arrived here in a pretty bad mood. I had heard bad things about the island and got overwhelmed by the noisiness, masses of tourists and groping in the street while going out for dinner in Patong. The hostel was nice though and I met Erik, a dutch guy I spent the next day with at the beach.

After two nights I moved to Karon and started my diving course. We did a training day in the pool and 6 dives in Racha Noi and Racha Yai over the next two days (although I almost overslept), it was amazing!

The vibe in Karon is much chiller, and Alix arrived from Seoul too so it's been fun. We relaxed at the beach and adopted Artemis, a cat who spent so much time with us she ended up shitting in our bed. Also found a super cool rasta restaurant I wish we'd encountered sooner!

I arrived in Bangkok around 3 A.M and spent the next day skating around town. I did the grand palace and the old town, crashing and going to bed early after a busy day.

The next day I met Arthur, a guy from Wilrijk who lives like 10 mins away, and some English people who I spent the day with. We were planning on doing some more temples but it started raining so we ended up wandering around a market. We passed by Khaosan road later, but Arthur and I decided we wanted more of a chill night so we ended up getting stoned in a laid back bar.

Today I woke up late and decided to do absolutely nothing before leaving for the airport at 4 p.m.

all_steps.name: Travel day: bangkok-Hongkong-Zurich
all_steps.description:
After traveling to the airport twice I boarded my flight to Hong kong. Yes twice... I missed my first flight because of good old Bangkok traffic (a supposedly 20min taxi ride took 2+ hours). I arrived about 42 minutes before the flight's departure time, leaving a slight chance of still making it but a 100% chance for trouble with my luggage. I decided to accept defeat and rebook the next morning to ensure the success of my masterplan (80 euros lost, but more preparation time and less security to pass due to the direct transit in Hongkong). 

To elaborate: my master plan is to skip the connecting flight from Zurich to Brussels, so I can visit Dirk in Lausanne. For this to work I have to ensure none of my luggage is checked in, or it will be sent to Brussels... With a small bag, a big 15 kg bag, a skateboard and a ticket without luggage included this proved to be a challenging task. 

I threw some stuff away and repacked to make my big bag lighter & seem smaller. I also devised a way to smuggle my skateboard through security (1) After the prep I went out to dinner and met a German couple on their honeymoon, the restaurant owner gave us free drinks and even joined us! (2) I spent the night in a hostel that sidehustles as a weed shop, but didn't sleep very well. 

Update: the plan worked perfectly, and I started hitchhiking to Lausanne after arriving in Zurich around 6 a.m. This brought me to one of the largest skateparks of Europe, but I ended up taking the train around 13 p.m because it didn't work out. In Lausanne I chilled in parque de Milan and spent the evening in a nice coffeebar waiting for Dirk.

I spent the first night fighting jet lag and going to The Great Escape bar with Dirk. The next day we skated around town, hillbombing to  Ouchy-Olympique spot to skate and go for a swim in the lake.  We went to sleep early to prepare for the next day's hike. 

In the morning we almost abandoned Jan, Dirk's German friend who was joining us for the hike, by missing the train. We made it though: walked up Pic Chaussy and decided to descend into les Diablerets, so we hiked about 10km far with 1km elevation. This was pretty tiring so we did a chill movie night after.

We were planning on doing a via feratta but it would be too expensive, so we ended up skating at Vidy skatepark all day yesterday. I felt a bit sick all day so it was probably for the best. 

Today we went for a goodbye brunch and visited the EPFL campus before I left for the airport. Nice couple of days to officially end my trip on!

all_steps.name: Vancouver + arrival at Big White
all_steps.description:
Loaded with a 22kg bag and a 25kg skibag (after taking out one pair of skis haha) (3&4) I hopped on the plane undercover. Platinum member "Ariel Raphael Rubin" gets into the lounge, gets extra legroom in the plane and doesn't have to pay for excess weight or oversized sports equipment hehe. 

My luggage got delayed though, so I didn't shower for the first couple of days and lived on 2 shirts and 3 pairs of socks for 5 days. KLM & insurance covered essential expenses though so I went on a little shopping spree. The luggage got lost for a bit before being located on a cargo flight to Montreal for some reason (5), but it just arrived!!! All good now and I got €1000 of free gear out of it (6)

After three days fixing admin, shopping and cruising around Vancouver I left for Kelowna at 19:40. I arrived on the mountain a bit later than the rest of the group but it cost me less and gave me more time in Vancouver.

Our apartment (7) is in the new staff building, it's super modern and spacy! We're 7 in the flat: it's a ski in/out place, close to the village centre and with a nice view (8) so very nois :))

We went out early for our first day on the slopes, but I had to buy some gear first (KLM's treat though) so I started late.  We got a couple laps in the trees, I dropped my phone of the lift (but found it instantly no worries) and snowboarded until the lifts closed at 15:30.

Today training started, I had to get used to my new skis (Faction Mana 2, as a very long pair of all mountain freestyle skis they feel very different than my usual racing skis) but it was nice exploring the whole mountain. Our instructor Ben is super chill and the group is mixed with interns from other organisations so good to meet some new people (1)

all_steps.name: Black Forest staff accom
all_steps.description:
It's been an awesome couple of weeks getting settled in at Big white!! We did almost get kicked out of our appartment thanks to Jack f-ing Keenan, but it's all good.

We met all the other interns, and spent 11 days training to get our skiing level on par, get to know the mountain and learn how to teach. Besides skiing like a noodle I was doing alright. 

Life in the village is not very eventful, but it's good fun: we spend all our evenings glued to the sofa watching a million movies per day and falling asleep (15-19), or out drinking at Sam's (20-23) (and I waste loads of time working on my uni applications of course). There are other cool things to do though: there is a departmental hockey league (go ski school steamersss), biweekly yoga classes and events like the staff christmas party last week.

Last weekend we passed our exam with Nick (21) and I am officially a CSIA ski instructor now!!!(2,3) We had a couple days off before starting work tomorrow and 30+ cm fell in the last couple of days so the mountain was amazingggggg today (4).

all_steps.name: Big White Village
all_steps.description:
Happy new yearrrrr,

We celebrated at the Snowminds people's place in Snowpines with all the interns yesterday. It's pretty far from our place but we managed to catch a lift in a shuttle. Then we ended the night at an after in Black Forest and a rave that was set up right by our door!

I started work, and a fair share of random stuff happened already. From 5 year olds crashing in the trees to weird anime kids singing on the chairlift, from a fun day with Parker who picked up skiing super fast (and almost landed me a request private lesson) to a fucked up day with a group of beginners of different abilities. All in all: I'm out on the mountain all day and work is fun :) I am dealing with all kinds of adult life bs (like tax and pension deductions) for the first time though, having a real job sucks lol.

In the last couple of weeks we did Christmas dinner with the roomies (5) and I finally went over to ski at Gem Lake and Falcon which was awesomeeee.  I did my first snowboard training session with Luke as well and I am gonna go for level 1 CASI. I took the biggest crash today though (folding in two against a tree while riding dragon's tongue with Owen) but I'm alright. 
Oeee and I finished all my master applications + got invited for the BAEF fellowship interview so that stuff is progressing as well :)

all_steps.name: 2 months at Biggie!
all_steps.description:
It's been two months at Biggie and life is great :)
I was just sick for 3 days though, the flu is going around >:(

The training for level 2 CSIA started (two days a week), and I'm working part time the rest of the week. I started hanging out with the EA lot more and got closer to some non intern people during Knoll sessions as well. The faculty hockey league started and I played in the first game but I could barely stay upright on my skates haha (3). Besides that we're still doing the same stuff all the time: ski instructor lunch at The Market, playing around in the snow and building forts with the kids (6), charcuter-ski on the mountain (7), getting dinner deals everywhere we can, going down to Kelowna city to watch a Hockey game or something when we're sick of the village (I drove!), and drinking at Sam's... A lot (4,5)

I have noticed my skiing is getting better! (With its ups and downs haha) I have more pressure control and angulation at slow speeds instead of just high speed carving all the time, but there is still some leaning in and shoulder compensation.

What else... Ah yeah, I broke my board trying a shitty backflip but the shop fixed it for 11eu (staffperks hehe). Oeeee I did land a backside 540 in a halfpipe which was crazy.

Jeeeeej got my level 2 ski certification today (2), the two exam days were a roller coaster, the warm temperatures ruined the snow this week, but we made it :).  We're celebrating by doing the smartest person of Big White quiz I made tonight. 

Besides the usual apartment clownery (16-21) a lot has happened: level 2 training, gossip table meetings, lots of kids and their shenanigans (9), the Stampede themed ski school prom (organised by yours truly with the social committee) (10), sunsets on our balcony (11),  Ebony's gender bender party(12), Knoll parties and aprés (13,15), I got better at hockey (16) and I started getting in to the park a little bit. 

I also went to Belgium for a week for the BAEF interview. Saw everyone <33 and went to Sacha's Bar Mitsvah, it was nice to be back for a bit. Also: I ended up getting selected for the BAEF grant, so I might be going to the US next year!!!  (Update: Berkeley here we comeeee)

About a week later dad came out to visit for ten days (6-8), we went out for dinner nearly everyday and he even came out to Sam's with the flat :). There was a cold spell where temperatures dropped to -26°C. But we got a lot of snow at the end of that week, so sick powder days the week after that (3-5). I sent a backflip on skis!! And I FINALLY landed my tamedog (at the Knoll!!) multiple times and on shit jumps so I have that trick down hehe. 

Anyway, still the same old fun stuff! I am excited for the last month up here :)

Overnight trip to Revelstoke!!

The conditions were shit but it was nice to see another resort. Revvie is insane! It's got the highest vertical drop in North America (2500 - 790) and some gnarly terrain. Farah and I explored the mountain while Emma and JL passed their level 3 exam!!! (2,3)

The village is cute too, although we didn't really get the chance to see much after getting locked out from the car lol. (5)

all_steps.name: Byebye biggie
all_steps.description:
115 dagen op de latten and the end of the season has come! Not surprisingly, I had snowrangers on my last day of work. 

In the last couple of weeks we did the hike and bev, I tried some more unsuccesfull wildcats and backflips, held the KC awards (Rohan got MIP!!) and danced on some funky tunes in the globe (so much better than Sams).

The second to last day we rented snowblades and did the liftie challenge! (Did it in jeans B-) ) Downing a drink on every chairlift sure gets you out of your shell while skiing. We ended the day with a charcuterski at the Knoll, before going to a bbq and sneaking into Altitunes! (22)

Besides that the last couple of days were typical spring skiing and I finally got in the park a little bit (23). Learned tubes on skis and got a miller flip on snowboard!! 

We did a huge clean up yesterday, I had a last dinner with Emma and played just dance with the flat before hopping on the shuttle to Kelowna today.

Onto the next adventure!

all_steps.name: Kelowna: start of the RV trip
all_steps.display_name: Kelowna: start of the RV trip
all_steps.description:
Spent the night in Kelowna, went for a dip in the hotel pool and some indian food, before flying to Vancouver (1) and picking up the RV (it's huge (3,4)). Luckily we were able to drop of some of our bags at their office because we had way too much stuff (2)

Oh yeah and Rohan and I started a parkour series, enjoy the first episode: PKBoys, the rise of legends (split in two because polarsteps only allows 1min vids)

1st night in the RV near Kamloops. We decided to skip on Whistler because of the detour and drove a bit more than expected, Anthony is killing it. We arrived around 8pm and had a chill night with creamy chicken pasta for dinner! 

Alsoooo, enjoy PKboys episode two: Kamloops edition

Wildcampspot with some nice views for tonight! (2-4)

We faced a closed road on the way and had do detour for an hour, but we persevered and managed to catch up to Ash, Rachelle, Sarah and Ryan to meet here! (1) Apparently Jens, Alex, Frida and Ella are also around so they'll be driving up here later tonight too!

Rohan is making rice for dinner and then we're having a chill night playing boardgames because it's raining outside. 

The next morning we had some RV trouble, but we figured out our tarp got stuck in the living room sliding mechanism, and cut it off to unblock it. We got to Revelstoke around 11 for a nice breakfast and stroll around town (5,6)

all_steps.name: Kicking Horse/Kinbasket lake
all_steps.description:
We drove up to Golden after our visit to Revvie, got stuck near a bridge again but we made it! The drive up was stunningggg (1). 

Time for camping chillings until Ryan and co join us here again. The campground has a driving range so we're going to play some golf (4,5) and tonight we're doing a chess tournament (I won from Rohan and Anthony hihi)

We got up early to drive out to Emerald lake in Yoho national park (4-7). We had some nice views and "penic scee's". There, we split up in two groups, one group was doing a small hike around the lake and the other group was doing a big hike up to Hamilton lake.

I joined the Hamilton lake group and thus swapped RV's for the afternoon hihi. It was cool but at one point the snow got to deep to traverse without snowshoes, so we had to turn back. (8-12)

After the hike we drove up to Lake louise to eat lunch and stroll around town for a bit, it looks like a sick mountain (13).

Then we drove to Banff, arriving at 18:30 at the astonishing campsite (1-3).

After chilling for a bit we headed into town (I got a lift from luis) for dinner at a korean place and drinks with everyone after (13-16)

Yesterday we drove back to lake louise to see the actual lake and it was stunningggg. You could walk all the way to the centre on the ice. (1,2)

Then we did the Johnston Canyon hike (3-6) to the lower waterfall, which was really funny because the trail was icy. We chilled in Banff again in the afternoon, before going back to the campsite. I found some guy with a skateboard in town, and skated for a bit for the first time in ages.

Back at the camping we played some footy& games and Rohan and I cooked Fajitas. Then we did some admin to fix the rest of our trip.

-

This morning we drove to Johnston lake (7-10) and walked around it (I found a tree shaped like a chair and decided to just chill there alone instead of walking all the way. It was sick).

Then we drove up to two jack lake (11) to see the water and we went back to the campsite for a chill afternoon.

We headed up to town for dinner, and spent the evening at an Irish pub in Banff village again.

Started the day early to get to Calgary at 10A.M, we had a meeting with Canadadream to fix our generator, before heading south to waterton lakes. Long drivedayyy, and surprise surprise: the only Camping in the are that's supposed to be open...is not. Not too surprising since the whole place is practically a ghosttown. It's so quiet the wildlife has even settled in town: we saw marmots and deer all over (3). We played basketball and soccer until we tired out.

We drove around town looking for a good spot next to the lake & bathrooms to park for the night. We ate Butter chicken and got drunk after, had a really nice chat with Eva near the river  :)). Buttttt the generator is busted again :/

-

Update: it dropped to negative temparatures overnight and snowed like 10cm, total change of scenery! 

The slides got stuck because of snow on the roof, so we had to find a creative solution... But it's 10:30 and we're on the road! (5)

Also enjoy PKboys episode 3: Nature is our playground

Although the roads are snowy and we had to throw away some eggs and oranges, we made it over the border easily (aside from Emily not knowing how fingerprint scanners work).

We drove down to Glacier with a supermarket/lunch pitstop in Browning and got there around 16 P.M. The campsite's bathrooms are closed for some reason... but it is located in a really peaceful forest next to lake mcdonald. We spent the afternoon discovering Apgar village, mainly its giftshop tho (I bought a cap, matching with Izzy!(2)) and I also found a sick local beer. (3) :)

-

Today we drove down Going-to-the-Sun road until its closure, then hiked further down it to get to John's Lake loop trailhead. We hiked about 7.5 km for 2.5h and passed two big waterfalls! I learned how to skip rocks (4) and saw a bunch of deer on the way.

Rachel, Ash, Sarah and Ryan caught up to us again so we're reunited! We ate spaghetti (9) and roasted marshmallows with a little party around our fire, before heading in to play Monopoly (10) (I lost, but managed to get some good trades in thanks to my shit-talking of course)

We were up early this morning, arriving at our campsite in the middle of nowhere near Helena around 2P.M. We finally have electricity again, and the showers here are amazingggg. 

We drove through the WoopWoop of Montana,  with a pitstop at Flathead lake for some rockskipping practice (4) and a pitstop at a Pawn Shop in Bigfork that had some random stuff (5,6). Also, I caught the Midwest virus and learned a new skill. (7)

To be considered a town in Montana you don't need much: a gas station, a casino, a church,  and if it's a good town a gun shop. 
The question is if we drove past more American flags or "Jesus is our saviour"/"Jesus died for the sins our world" roadsigns (2,3).

Eva&Izzy made bangers&mash (8) while the rest of us played monopoly again (this time I destroyed everyone, and Emily and I got in a fight lol). We ate dinner around the fire and stargazed for the rest of the night.

We drove three hours to Mammoth campground in Yellowstone, only then realizing I messed up by booking the wrong campsite and we didn't have a place to sleep. Hands down the stupidest thing I've done in a while... 

Anyhoooo decided to skip on Yellowstone tomorrow and just did the hot springs (1-7) for like 40mins before driving 2 hours (with some bison traffic) back up to Bozeman (we were over our km's and kind of short on time anyway, but it still sucked). We booked a last minute fancy ass campsite with pools and stuff, and Rohan and I cooked spicy teriyaki pulled chicken burgers (9) (easily the best meal of the trip). We went to the pool (8) and sauna after dinner (overhearing very cringe American teen conversations around us), did laundry (10) together and hade an amazing smoothie. The other campsite refunded me too, so yesterday didn't end up being too bad.

-

We went to the pool again this morning and left around 11A.M, onto the next destination!

Booking the campsite in Lolo, Montana (for 20/04) was a hassle, my payment wouldn't come through until I played the US national anthem and saluted my phone with Anthony.

There wasn't much to see on the drive up here either. We played some games in the van and had a pitstop in Missoula where we saw the most Mid-West American woman ever (will never see a bigger belt buckle).

Eva and Izzy made nachos for dinner, Rohan and I went to explore the area and climb some rocks and we all played some poker around the fire until it got dark (1-3).

-

The next day we drove about three hours, finally crossing the border into Idaho. We broke up the drive by going bowling for a couple hours (4), I suck but it was a lot of fun. We also did a pitstop dedicated to Eva: in the biggest gift shop of Montana, not surprisingly it sucked but it was funny. We arrived at our campsite in Lincoln around 5 P.M and had another chill night after dinner (chicken pasta by Rohan and Emily)

-

Big driving day again!! We want to get in most of the driving so we can stay in the same place for a couple nights at the end of our trip.

We left around 9A.M and drove over four hours today (evidently, loads of codenames was played), with a pitstop in a Goodwill (5) where I bought a vest (it's like a Walmart type beat thrift store, not the vibe). Our next pitstop was in a park near Moses Lake, the sun came out and we had a lovely picknick!! (6)

We arrived at Lake Easton RV park around 3P.M to explore the surroundings and play some badminton before dinner (butter chicken rice by Eva, Izzy and I)

-

I woke up at 7:00A.M with Rohan and Emily to go for a morning run, did 5k (high effort and pretty slow, but satisfied with my first run in like 8 months). 

Departure at 9:30A.M, with only a 2/3 hour drive scheduled today this leaves enough time to explore several villages on the way!

We stopped in Bellevue (9) (city connected to Seattle) to do some American stuff! We went to trader Joe's, Chick fil A and Target and walked around town before hitting the road again.

Lastly we drove through Mt Vernon, a cute town with tulip farms, before arriving at Skagit River campsite around, where we will be staying a couple nights :). We chilled in the sun & I fell asleep reading. Rohan and Eva had a cook off to see who can make the best grilled burger (10), and then we went to bed.

all_steps.name: Mt Baker
all_steps.description:
We  went for another big walk today. The plan was to do Montauk peak trail, but the drive up was so sketchy that we turned back. We ended up on a random trail along the river. We took a swim break, found gold in the sand??? and we saw a bunch of snakes. Back at the campsite we had a chill gamenight and Izzy made chicken pasta. 

- 

Early wake up for our last full day! We drove to Bellingham to stroll through Fairhaven neighbourhood of this cute boujee town. We had a fancy lunch and grabbed a coffee before driving to our last campsite at Mount Baker. Dinner was an improvised pasta salad with all our leftovers that was surprisingly good. We spent our last night together at a sports bar nearby. 

-

Early wake up this morning to pack up & clean.
Off to Canadream now!

Matt and I dropped off our stuff at his hotel before heading into town and meeting up with the others. We spent the day in Vancouver daydrinking and going to bookstores tipsy to find a gift for Emma's parents. 

It wasn't long before I had to jump on the metro towards the airport and say goodbye. :((

-

I had an overnight flight to Kitchener that kinda sucked. But smiles quickly returned when Emma picked me up to drive straight to Niagara falls.
The falls were cool but it's a weird ass village: it's more of an amusement park than anything else. We had a cute pick nick, and I fell asleep on the drive back to her house in Moffat. We walked her dog Miga, ate roasted hotdogs, played basketball and had a lovely pita dinner with her parents before heading to bed early.

-

The next day started early, when Emma's mom dropped us off in Oakville. We took the train to Union station in Toronto to spend half a day there. We walked around old town, the harbour & downtown before hopping on the 15:30 train to Montreal. The train had a slight delay but it was quite enjoyable, we solved riddles, Emma napped, I called mom and dad before arriving at Ava's place in Montreal around 21:00.

Upon arrival at Ava's we met Sarah and Tom, and we talked the night away with some tea (the neighbors even complained about 'noise' at 1:30).
Emma and I are sleeping on mats on the floor but we're tired enough to make it work!

-
The next day Ava was our tourguide. She walked us around her neighbourhood Mont Royale city, we hiked up the park hill to see the view of the city, went to see her lab and random artifacts at McGill, sneaked into the student Open Air Pub for a hot dog and live music (in the rain), and went for dinner at an Argentinian place for Arepas.
This amounted to 16km of walking!
At night we had some drinks at home and watched the Leafs game.

-

The next morning we went for a breakfast Bagel and had Brunch with Ava. Emma treated me to my first time at Tims, where the VERY old server flirted with me and the iced coffee was disgusting. We went to the BioDome after to see the botanical garden and stuff before heading back to the city to swap appartments. After logging all my stuff to our new place we went for dinner at Les 3 Brasseurs and watched the Montreal Canadiens playoff game. We met up with Ava at an Irish pub after and forgot to give back her keys oopsie.

-

For our last day we strolled around Old Montreal and chilled in a park near the harbour. We had an awesome lunch and enjoyed the sun. We ended the day with Poutine, and drinks + movienight at our place.

-

Time to go! Big travel day ahead, and my bus leaves at 8A.M. 
It is officially time to say byebye Canadaaaa, thanks for this amazing winter <33

Early wake up to catch my 8 A.M bus, it was exhausting because the air-conditioning was broken and the border crossing took pretty long. I had a 3 hour layover in Concord. I called mom, Eva and Emma to pass the time, which was really nice. The second bus to Conway was more comfortable, and the Uber driver bringing me and my gazillion bags up to our cottage was very friendly. I had some time to myself, as everyone else arrived later/went to bed early.

-

The next day I woke up insanely late to recuperate (like 11:30). Having skipped breakfast I promptly hopped on a kayak tour the lake in front of the cottage with Emma, Max and Manuela. It started raining, but it was a really nice vibe and it was a great way to get to know Charlotte's friends. As it was raining we relaxed inside the rest of the day, stuffing ourselves with countless snacks and discussing ethical dillemas. We ended the day with a big pasta dinner to carb up for the Marathon. And after the runners went to sleep, we secretly made some supportive signs for the race.

-

Wake up at 6:45 A.M and departure at 7 this morning!!

Finding a spot to cheer proved to be a hard task. Officially, you can only spectate from the finish line. Nonetheless, we managed to find a spot at mile 21 and got some funny reactions to our signs.

Both Fanny and Allie finished sub 4h, and after a celebratory lunch we embarked on our 6 hour drive back to NYC

I got up early for my only full day in the city! I met up with Andy and his friend Joe to do the Intrepid museum and have lunch at a Peruvian place. Intrepid is this aircraft carrier that served through WW2, the Cold war and the Vietnam war. It holds a lot of cool aircrafts and a submarine+ tells the story of how the boat operated. After that Andy and I continued our museum journey with the National History Museum, where I spent the most time in the room with rocks (lol nerd). 

At seven I met up with Charlotte for a testimony about 07/10, which was harsh but good. We went for dinner&tea after at a Middle Eastern place where we got to talking with this famous Israeli choreographer super randomly (classic NYC moment).

-

I met up with Andy again today in Williamsburg. We walked around town, went to trader Joe's and Domino park and then had lunch at an Aussie place called Hole In The Wall. 

I said bye to him around 2 and left for the airport. Our little upgrade cheatcode didn't work this time, but I managed to get out of paying for an extra bag anywayyyy. 

JFK is a mess, so the flight is a bit delayed, but off we go!!! It's been a great trip :)

After three weeks back in Europe; mainly working in Antwerp, fixing Berkeley stuff and the occasional party in Delft it was time for vacay again! 

Emma visited for two weeks and we touristed around Belgium & The Netherlands with a busy itinerary including Antwerp, Knokke, Brugge, Gent, Brussels, Delft, The Hague, Utrecht and Amsterdam. Being as forgetful as can be and dropping house keys in the elevator shaft, drinking a gazillion coffees and doing endless beer tastings, many town halls and churches, canals and bikes, ... In other words the full Belgian/Dutch experience!

8 day family trip to Sicily!

We stayed in a really cool villa on a hill, where a light breeze mellowed out the heatwave. Although there were insanely loud crickets, it was super relaxed. 

We visited Catania, Cassibile, Syracuse (saw the biggest Mikveh in Europe, super old), Notto, a weird archeologic/sculpture park and had loads of beach days (some of them wild and stinky, other with annoying organised aquagym or "plastic" algae).

all_steps.name: Dour
all_steps.description:
Overvolle auto en fancy kamperen, foodpreppen en teveel alcohol kopen, kapotte stoelen en matras-zonsessies, ruiltochten en blind dates, windmolens en afterparties.
Waar heb je nog nood aan?

Beach volleyball, climbing, craft beer bars, ice cream, Indian places, loads of boardgames and epic cooking sessions.

A.k.a the IBM intern experience.

all_steps.name: Afscheidsfeestje
all_steps.description:
Byebye Antwerpen!! 

Na een supergezellig (last minute) afscheidsfeest waarbij eindelijk mijn twee werelden zijn samengekomen, komt het jaar officieel ten einde!!

Veel gezien, veel gedaan, veel ontmoet,... Op naar het volgende hoofdstuk!!
